description: An iron-based alloy whose carbon and treatment give it a chosen set of properties is 'teshilo'. Other alloying elements may also be present. 'tesu' (iron) names the base element; an exact steel grade identifies the composition and processing that a particular use requires.
articulatory_notes: Dental 't' closes behind the upper teeth and releases into front 'e'. The tongue blade narrows the channel for stressed unvoiced 'shi'; lateral 'l' then carries the voice into final rounded 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The opening dental 'te' echoes 'tesu' (iron). The following 'shi.lo' lengthens that familiar start through a hiss and lateral release, so steel sounds related without sounding identical.
usage_notes: Grades matter where performance matters. A construction or safety record can give the composition, treatment, coating, and applicable standard.

semantic_domains.creation: an iron alloy selected and treated for made structures and tools
semantic_domains.physical: an iron-based alloy whose properties depend on composition and processing
